steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries question. After the blank, the text continues: "by Japanese violinist Shinichi Suzuki, who sought to parallel the linguistic learning environment, the method emphasizes..." That second clause ("the method emphasizes...") is an independent clause with its own subject. The blank must create a boundary that prevents a comma splice between the two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — tenets developed: "Developed by Japanese violinist Shinichi Suzuki, who sought to parallel the linguistic learning environment" becomes a modifier attached to "tenets," but then "the method emphasizes" is a second independent clause joined to the first with only a comma—a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — tenets, developed: The comma before "developed" sets off a participial phrase, but the sentence still runs both independent clauses together without a proper boundary. This is still a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — tenets. Developed: The period ends the first sentence at "tenets." The second sentence then opens with the participial phrase "Developed by Japanese violinist Shinichi Suzuki, who sought to parallel the linguistic learning environment," which correctly modifies the subject "the method" that follows. Two complete sentences, no boundary error. ✓
  • Choice D — tenets that, developed: Inserting "that" creates a relative clause without a verb ("tenets that, developed by Suzuki, who sought..."), leaving the first independent clause without a complete predicate. This is ungrammatical. ✗
That matches Choice C — tenets. Developed.
